expanded_papers.title: Face Mask Wearing Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLO-v4
expanded_papers.abstract: To solve the problems of low accuracy, low real-time performance, poor robustness and others caused by the complex environment, this paper proposes a face mask recognition and standard wear detection algorithm based on the improved YOLO-v4. Firstly, an improved CSPDarkNet53 is introduced into the trunk feature extraction network, which reduces the computing cost of the network and improves the learning ability of the model. Secondly, the adaptive image scaling algorithm can reduce computation and redundancy effectively. Thirdly, the improved PANet structure is introduced so that the network has more semantic information in the feature layer. At last, a face mask detection data set is made according to the standard wearing of masks. Based on the object detection algorithm of deep learning, a variety of evaluation indexes are compared to evaluate the effectiveness of the model. The results of the comparations show that the mAP of face mask recognition can reach 98.3% and the frame rate is high at 54.57 FPS, which are more accurate compared with the exiting algorithm.
expanded_papers.expanded_from_seed: CR-YOLOv8: Multiscale Object Detection in Traffic Sign Images

expanded_papers.title: Sparse R-CNN: End-to-End Object Detection with Learnable Proposals
expanded_papers.abstract: We present Sparse R-CNN, a purely sparse method for object detection in images. Existing works on object detection heavily rely on dense object candidates, such as k anchor boxes pre-defined on all grids of image feature map of size H × W. In our method, however, a fixed sparse set of learned object proposals, total length of N, are provided to object recognition head to perform classification and location. By eliminating HWk (up to hundreds of thousands) hand-designed object candidates to N (e.g. 100) learnable proposals, Sparse R-CNN completely avoids all efforts related to object candidates design and many-to-one label assignment. More importantly, final predictions are directly output without non-maximum suppression post-procedure. Sparse R-CNN demonstrates accuracy, run-time and training convergence performance on par with the well-established detector baselines on the challenging COCO dataset, e.g., achieving 45.0 AP in standard 3× training schedule and running at 22 fps using ResNet-50 FPN model. We hope our work could inspire re-thinking the convention of dense prior in object detectors. The code is available at: https://github.com/PeizeSun/SparseR-CNN.
expanded_papers.expanded_from_seed: CR-YOLOv8: Multiscale Object Detection in Traffic Sign Images

expanded_papers.title: Scaled-YOLOv4: Scaling Cross Stage Partial Network
expanded_papers.abstract: We show that the YOLOv4 object detection neural network based on the CSP approach, scales both up and down and is applicable to small and large networks while maintaining optimal speed and accuracy. We propose a network scaling approach that modifies not only the depth, width, resolution, but also structure of the network. YOLOv4-large model achieves state-of-the-art results: 55.5% AP (73.4% AP50) for the MS COCO dataset at a speed of ~ 16 FPS on Tesla V100, while with the test time augmentation, YOLOv4-large achieves 56.0% AP (73.3 AP50). To the best of our knowledge, this is currently the highest accuracy on the COCO dataset among any published work. The YOLOv4-tiny model achieves 22.0% AP (42.0% AP50) at a speed of ~443 FPS on RTX 2080Ti, while by using TensorRT, batch size = 4 and FP16-precision the YOLOv4-tiny achieves 1774 FPS.
expanded_papers.expanded_from_seed: CR-YOLOv8: Multiscale Object Detection in Traffic Sign Images

expanded_papers.title: An Improved YOLO11n-Based Algorithm for Road Sign Detection
expanded_papers.abstract: Highlights What are the main findings? To improve the performance of road sign detection algorithms in complex backgrounds for detecting multi-scale, low-resolution, and occluded small targets, a Multi-path Gated Aggregation module is designed. This module consists of a multi-scale feature extraction branch, a color feature extraction branch, and a detail feature extraction branch. In order to enhance the detection performance of the YOLO11n algorithm for small and blurry targets, high-resolution information from the shallow layers of the Backbone is integrated into the Neck network. Moreover, a Group Convolution-Layer Normalization-SiLU (GLS) structure is designed in the SPPF structure to achieve the fusion of information at different levels during SPPF information transmission. What are the implications of the main findings? A modular design paradigm is proposed for the feature extraction from multi-scale, low-resolution, and small targets in complex backgrounds. The introduced channel interaction mechanism and GLS-enhanced SPPF structure provide reusable structural references and design insights for improving the robustness of target detection under occlusion, blurring, low-resolution, and multi-scale variation in complex scenes. It attains a high balance between detection accuracy and real-time performance, making it suitable for vehicle deployment. While maintaining a high frame rate of 134 FPS, this method significantly improves the detection accuracy of various road signs (especially for low-resolution, partially occluded, and small targets) and can meet the dual requirements of reliability and real-time performance of intelligent driving systems in real and complex scenes. Abstract For vehicle driving scenarios in complex backgrounds, road sign detection faces challenges such as multi-scale targets, long-distances, and low-resolution. To address these challenges, a detection method based on an improved YOLO11n network is proposed. Firstly, to accommodate the multi-scale characteristics of the targets and improve the network’s ability to detect low-resolution objects and details, a Multi-path Gated Aggregation (MGA) Module is proposed, achieving these objectives via multi-dimensional feature extraction. Secondly, the Neck is improved by designing a network structure that incorporates high-resolution information from the Backbone, thereby enhancing the detection capabilities for small and blurry targets. Finally, an enhanced Spatial Pyramid Pooling—Fast (SPPF) module is proposed, wherein a Group Convolution-Layer Normalization-SiLU structure is integrated across various stages of information passing. By fusing adjacent channel information, it effectively suppresses complex background noise across multiple scales and amplifies road marking features, which consequently boosts the model’s discriminability for distant and obscured targets. Experimental results on a multi-type road sign dataset show that the improved model achieves an mAP@0.5 of 96.96%, which is 1.42% higher than the original model. The mAP@0.5–0.95 and Recall rates are 83.94% and 92.94%, respectively, while the inference speed remains at 134 FPS. Research demonstrates that via targeted modular designs, the proposed approach strikes a superior balance between detection accuracy and real-time efficiency. Consequently, it provides robust technical support for the reliable operation of intelligent vehicle perception systems under complex conditions.
expanded_papers.expanded_from_seed: CR-YOLOv8: Multiscale Object Detection in Traffic Sign Images

expanded_papers.title: Gmw-Yolo: Enhanced Vehicle and Pedestrian Detection in Foggy Conditions
expanded_papers.abstract: To address the challenges of object detection in foggy environments-such as difficult target perception, severe occlusion, and frequent false and missed detections caused by low visibility-we propose GMW-YOLO, a fog-oriented object detection algorithm based on YOLOv11. Firstly, we design a Global Edge Information Aggregation (GEIA) module that integrates edge cues into multi-scale feature representations, enhancing the network's attention to object boundaries. Secondly, aiming at the limitation of fixed receptive fields in conventional convolution, which impairs the simultaneous extraction of small-object fine details and global information, we propose a MultiScale Receptive Field Sharing Convolution (MSRFConv) module. It achieves efficient and consistent information aggregation spanning local details to global receptive fields in single-scale features, and effectively strengthens the feature representation of small objects.Finally, Wise-IoU v3 is adopted as the regression loss, where its dynamic weight adjustment and boundary-sensitive optimization mechanism effectively mitigate boundary misalignment and reduce regression errors for small targets. Experimental results on the RTTS dataset demonstrate that, compared with YOLOv11, GMW-YOLO achieves $\mathbf{7 0. 7 \%}$ mAP@50 and $\mathbf{4 6. 7 \%}$ mAP@50:95, improving by 3.0 and 3.3 percentage points, respectively, thereby validating the enhanced adaptability and effectiveness of the proposed method in foggy scenarios.

expanded_papers.title: SRL-YOLOv8: Lightweight model for small traffic signs in complex environments
expanded_papers.abstract: Automatic traffic sign detection holds significant application value in intelligent driving systems. In recent years, with the advancement of deep learning, traffic sign detection technology has made remarkable progress. However, detecting small-scale, long-distance, and background-interference signs remains challenging in real-world complex scenarios. This paper proposes a lightweight and efficient detection model, SRL-YOLOv8n, which significantly improves small-target detection accuracy while maintaining minimal parameters. First, the SPD-Conv module is introduced to enhance spatial detail perception and improve small-target feature representation. Second, a cross-level fusion architecture is constructed to fully integrate low-level details with high-level semantic information, thereby strengthening multi-scale detection capabilities. Finally, the C2f module is improved and a shared-convolution detection head architecture (LSCD) is proposed to optimize computational efficiency. Experimental results on the TT100K public dataset demonstrate that this method achieves an mAP@50 of 89.2% with only 1.65 million parameters, outperforming mainstream lightweight models. This approach achieves an optimal balance between precision and efficiency, providing a practical solution for edge device deployment.

expanded_papers.title: Implementation Real Time Speed Control Enhanced Sign Detection
expanded_papers.abstract: Road safety is a major concern in modern transportation, with traffic sign detection essential for guiding drivers and controlling vehicle speed. Manual observation of signs like speed limits, school zones, and pedestrians is often unreliable due to human error. This project introduces a real-time speed control system integrated with an enhanced traffic sign detection model using deep learning. The system employs the YOLOv11 algorithm to detect signs accurately and uses a proposed DeepCAS-LSTM (Deep CNN with Channel and Spatial attention+LSTM) for classification. For real-world deployment, models were optimized using TensorFlow Lite and the NCNN framework, improving performance on embedded systems. A custom dataset with 1,453 labeled images was used for detection and 1,642 cropped images for classification. The detection model achieved a mAP@50 of 0.909, while the DeepCAS-LSTM reached $100 \%$ training and $91.54 \%$ testing accuracy. Inference time was reduced from 10 to 2 seconds. The DeepCAS-LSTM was also compared with AlexNet, GoogLeNet, and VGGNet, demonstrating its reliability in intelligent transport systems.

expanded_papers.title: YOLOv8 Model for Traffic Junction Analysis
expanded_papers.abstract: The paper presents an automated traffic detection system based on YOLOv8 model. The system allows accurate counting of cars at junctions and generates $\mathbf{2 4}$-hour traffic volume data. It uses a YOLOv8 model trained specifically for the purpose of tracking and identifying vehicles, which it groups by hour. The results are stored in a CSV file that matches the structure of American Highway Data, so that it is compatible with existing traffic optimization software. This method is implemented to enable the development of traffic signal control and urban planning by data driven processes. The system accurately measures traffic flow as it achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP@ 50) of 75.86 %. Detection performance of the model was 89.09 % precision and 72.27 % recall, showing strong detection capabilities. It is shown to be capable of enhancing traffic performance and reducing congestion through the delivery of trustworthy vehicle identification and counting abilities.

expanded_papers.title: A Multicomponent Cross-Integration Network for Traffic Sign Detection in Complex Conditions
expanded_papers.abstract: The accurate detection and recognition of traffic signs are crucial for autonomous driving, advanced driver assistance systems, and road safety. In real-world road scenarios with complex backgrounds, traffic sign detection often faces challenges, leading to false and missed detections. This article introduces an innovative network MCCI-Net to address the challenges associated with recognizing and detecting traffic signs, considering their vulnerability to perspective distortion and illumination interference. First, a CNN-based backbone network with feature grouping was designed to improve the accuracy and efficiency of feature extraction. Second, a transformer encoder with an interactive fusion architecture was designed to enhance key features, refine and balance the correlation between semantic and location information, and gain the ability to mitigate feature conflicts. Finally, a transformer-decoder head is designed to optimize object queries for category prediction and box regression, enhancing detection accuracy and iteration speed reliability. Experimental results fully validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed MCCI-Net in traffic sign detection and recognition. The approach demonstrates strong capabilities in overcoming the challenges posed by perspective distortion and illumination interference. It exhibits superior performance compared with the existing state-of-the-art traffic sign detection methods. Our approach achieved a 97.2% in the mAP@0.5 evaluation on the public dataset TT100K, 97.5% on the CCTSDB dataset, and 94.1% on the GTSDB dataset.

expanded_papers.title: STDE-YOLOv5s: a traffic sign detection algorithm based on context information enhancement
expanded_papers.abstract: With the rapid development of autonomous driving technology, traffic sign detection has become a crucial component. Existing object detection networks encounter difficulties in accurately identifying small sized traffic signs. Considering the strong capability of the YOLOv5s algorithm in small object detection, its relatively low number of parameters, and ease of deployment, this paper selects YOLOv5s as the base model and proposes an improved traffic sign detection algorithm. Firstly, the feature fusion network is improved. By adding horizontal connection paths and a weighted fusion mechanism, the efficiency of multi scale feature fusion is enhanced. Additionally, by incorporating more backbone feature information into the Neck layer, the loss of detailed information is reduced, making small sized traffic signs more distinct in the images. Finally, a Context Information Enhanced Module that combines dilated convolution and deformable convolution is proposed, which enhances the network’s ability to extract context information. The proposed improved algorithm has achieved excellent results on the TT100K dataset. Its mAP50 outperforms existing advanced models such as YOLOv8 and YOLOv11.

expanded_papers.title: Improved algorithm for visually impaired individuals avoid obstacles based on YOLOv8
expanded_papers.abstract: The use of object detection technology to assist visually impaired individuals in obstacle avoidance has gained increasing attention. However, in real-world scenarios, the scale variation of obstacles and the prevalence of small-sized obstacles pose significant challenges. This paper proposes a novel deep learning-based method for assisting the visually impaired in obstacle avoidance, which combines the YOLOv8 model, the EMA attention mechanism, WTConv technology, and an enlarged 160x160 object detection head to improve obstacle perception capabilities in complex environments. YOLOv8, as the baseline model for object detection, enables real-time perception, while the EMA attention mechanism selectively enhances important features, reducing noise and improving detection robustness under varying lighting conditions and occlusions. Furthermore, WTConv, through wavelet transformation, captures multi-scale spatial information, thereby enhancing the representation of fine details in low-resolution inputs and improving the accuracy of obstacle detection. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed EMA-YOLOv8 model achieves an accuracy of 89.3% in complex real-world scenarios, outperforming the baseline model by 3.7%, highlighting its promising application in improving the safety and assistive technology for visually impaired individuals.

expanded_papers.title: BanglaRoadSignNet: A YOLO-Based Framework for Efficient Road Sign Detection and Recognition in Bangladesh
expanded_papers.abstract: In developing countries like Bangladesh, the detection and recognition of road signs is a key component of intelligent transportation systems (ITS). This study addresses unique road signs and nonstandardized signage challenges, improving the detection and recognition process of traffic signs. The study highlights the increasing vehicular traffic and the lack of automation in traffic monitoring, which could improve roadway security and traffic management. This paper proposes BanglaRoadSignNet, a robust framework leveraging YOLOv11 (You Only Look Once) for accurate and simultaneous detection and recognition of road signs mainly for Bangladesh roads. BanglaRoadSignNet establishes a link between existing identification frameworks by addressing multilingual signage, occlusions, and environmental conditions, achieving high performance. The proposed model demonstrates significant performance with a precision of 94.3%, recall of 98.8%, mAP50 of 99.0%, and mAP50-95 of 92.6%, surpassing earlier versions of YOLO. The study’s major contributions are the novel architecture of YOLOv11 on a Bangladesh local dataset and its accuracy and reliability in detecting road signs, making it a practical solution for real-time ITS applications in Bangladesh.

expanded_papers.title: Toward Safe Driving: Efficient Detection of Small Blurred Signs in Real-World Scenarios
expanded_papers.abstract: Accurate traffic sign recognition is critical for safe driving, as over half of traffic accidents stem from drivers’ negligence of traffic signs. Thus, developing robust traffic sign detection methods is essential to improve road safety. While existing object detection methods have achieved remarkable success, their performance in traffic sign detection is often limited by small object sizes and low-resolution appearances. To address these issues, this study proposes a novel traffic sign detection framework with three innovative components for precise localization and classification: 1) a hierarchical feature aggregation module that emphasizes high-level semantic information for traffic sign localization; 2) a cross-layer semantic residual network that enhances recognition of small and blurred signs via hierarchical feature interaction and fusion; 3) a lightweight feature alignment unit that bridges semantic gaps between cross-level representations. These components jointly tackle the challenges of detecting small and blurred traffic signs in real-world driving scenarios. Experiments were conducted on three public datasets (TT100K, CCTSDB2021, GTSDB). Results show the proposed model outperforms other methods with comparable parameters. Additionally, dynamic motion blur augmentation was applied to datasets to simulate real driving scenarios, and experiments confirm the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance under such challenging conditions. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/Mo7nex/SAttFusion-YOLO

expanded_papers.title: StormGuard YOLO: Real-Time Sign Detection for Adverse Weather Conditions
expanded_papers.abstract: Over 38,700 car crashes occur each year causing around 600 deaths. In addition, approximately 16,300 people sustain injuries due to adverse weather conditions. This type of weather can come in the form of rain, fog, high sunshine, or overcast skies, causing a reduction in performance in traffic sign detection systems of self-driving vehicles. To solve this problem, the study proposes a model that detects various traffic signs in adverse weather conditions. The study employs Cycle Consistent Generative Adversarial Network (Cycle-GAN) for conversion of clear weather image into various other weather forms. This approach is used to train YOLO (You Only Look Once) detector, so that it learns robust features across a variety of environments. The augmented data provides model with a better performance. Further, the YOLO architecture can be fine-tuned on both, the real and augmented data sets. This has been done to optimize the model's performance in a timely manner without reducing detection accuracy. Results indicate that using Cycle-GANs has improved the mean average precision (mAP) and recall of the YOLO architecture when compared with other YOLO architectures. The combination of generative and discriminative modeling is established to be an effective, scalable solution to Intelligent Transportation Systems that operate in the real world.

expanded_papers.title: Traffic Sign Recognition in Autonomous Driving: Dataset, Benchmark, and Field Experiment
expanded_papers.abstract: Traffic Sign Recognition (TSR) is a core perception capability for autonomous driving, where robustness to cross-region variation, long-tailed categories, and semantic ambiguity is essential for reliable real-world deployment. Despite steady progress in recognition accuracy, existing traffic sign datasets and benchmarks offer limited diagnostic insight into how different modeling paradigms behave under these practical challenges. We present TS-1M, a large-scale and globally diverse traffic sign dataset comprising over one million real-world images across 454 standardized categories, together with a diagnostic benchmark designed to analyze model capability boundaries. Beyond standard train-test evaluation, we provide a suite of challenge-oriented settings, including cross-region recognition, rare-class identification, low-clarity robustness, and semantic text understanding, enabling systematic and fine-grained assessment of modern TSR models. Using TS-1M, we conduct a unified benchmark across three representative learning paradigms: classical supervised models, self-supervised pretrained models, and multimodal vision-language models (VLMs). Our analysis reveals consistent paradigm-dependent behaviors, showing that semantic alignment is a key factor for cross-region generalization and rare-category recognition, while purely visual models remain sensitive to appearance shift and data imbalance. Finally, we validate the practical relevance of TS-1M through real-scene autonomous driving experiments, where traffic sign recognition is integrated with semantic reasoning and spatial localization to support map-level decision constraints. Overall, TS-1M establishes a reference-level diagnostic benchmark for TSR and provides principled insights into robust and semantic-aware traffic sign perception. Project page: https://guoyangzhao.github.io/projects/ts1m.

expanded_papers.title: Learning Distortion-Aware Correlation Filters With Target Mask for UAV Tracking
expanded_papers.abstract: Recently, due to the excellent computational efficiency and interpretability, discriminative correlation filter (DCF)-based tracking methods have received extensive attention in the field of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). However, existing methods are usually susceptible to interference from significant appearance changes of the target object or background occlusion, which leads to tracking failure. To effectively address these issues, we propose a distortion-aware correlation filter with target mask (DACFTM) for UAV, which introduces a target regularization term to enhance the target perception ability of the tracking model. Specifically, we construct a target mask matrix based on the highest peak of the response map of the previous frame, thereby leveraging prior reliable localization confidence, and multiply it with the current feature map to obtain a regularization term containing only target information, effectively distinguishing the target from the background. In addition, to deal with tracking failure caused by large appearance changes, we propose a distortion-aware mechanism. When the quality of the response map corresponding to the filter is higher than a set threshold, we consider the filter is reliable and adopt the filter fusion strategy; otherwise, the saved high-quality filter is selected for the tracking in the next frame. Finally, we comprehensively evaluate the performance of DACFTM on three mainstream UAV benchmark datasets, and experimental results demonstrate that the DACFTM achieves impressive tracking performance.

expanded_papers.title: A Review of Techniques, Indicators and Devices for Traffic Congestion Monitoring
expanded_papers.abstract: Road transport undeniably constitutes the predominant mechanism for facilitating the transportation of both goods and individuals on a global scale, serving as an essential backbone for economic and social interactions across diverse regions and cultures. The noticeable decrease in the flow of vehicles, which can be attributed to a plethora of internal and external factors, with a particular emphasis on the phenomenon of congestion, has profound implications that significantly influence fuel consumption rates, contribute to pollution associated with emissions, adversely affect the health and well-being of bystanders, and culminate in a considerable loss of time for individuals navigating these congested environments. In light of their elevated population densities coupled with their classification as emerging economies, South Asian countries find themselves necessitated to implement automated systems for the critical processes of predicting, identifying, and effectively addressing the challenges posed by road traffic congestion in order to enhance urban mobility and overall transport efficiency. This thorough research carefully explores the various techniques that have been utilized to recognize traffic congestion, presenting an extensive assessment of their individual strengths and weaknesses, thus offering insightful observations about the existing situation in this field of study. The examination of the diverse approaches and advanced technologies that have been utilized for the operation of lane-less roadways have been conducted, revealing substantial potential for further innovations that could greatly assist future researchers in their endeavors to enhance traffic management and improve roadway safety and efficiency.

expanded_papers.title: Advances in Traffic Sign Recognition for Autonomous Driving: A Systematic Literature Review (2014-2024)
expanded_papers.abstract: Real-time recognition of traffic is essential to enhance safety and efficiency in autonomous vehicles. This systematic review (2014-2024) assesses progress in recognition systems using deep learning, emphasizing on technologies like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Region-Based CNN (RCNN), and YOLO variants, YOLOv8. Autonomous driving, Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), and Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADASx) are the main applications. The survey indicates limitations in handling degraded signs, geographical robustness, and weather extremity robustness. Future work needs to concentrate on standardized data sets, light-weight architectures (e.g., YOLO optimizations), and global traffic sign variation support. This combination provides a roadmap for facilitating robust, real-time recognition capability, which is critical to autonomous driving safety and smart transportation systems.

expanded_papers.title: An Experimental Study on Road Condition Monitoring and Junction Analysis Using Bus Trajectory Data
expanded_papers.abstract: Road network provides essential connectivity to passengers and commercial freight. Road surface quality, junction types (circular/crossroad), and their regulations [traffic light (TL)-controlled/uncontrolled (UC)] significantly affect vehicle movement—poor road quality and inefficient junction regulations lead to increased congestion and accidents. In developing countries, road network monitoring is difficult due to limited sensing infrastructure, and dynamic conditions such as different road surface quality across lanes and time-varying junction regulations. Existing solutions that rely on threshold-based methods and supervised learning require frequent recalibration and labeled datasets, which can be time-consuming and error-prone in such dynamic conditions. Public transportation buses traverse the major part of the road network. They can be used as mobile sensors for large-scale road network monitoring. In this experimental study, we opportunistically collect the bus trajectories consisting of GPS, accelerometer, and gyroscope data in multiple cities of India. The unlabelled trajectories are processed using self-organizing map (SOM) and k-means algorithms to assess road surface condition, and DBSCAN and k-means algorithms with novel centroid initialization are used to infer the junction location, type, and time-varying regulation. Experimental results show that the inferred surface roughness (low/medium/high/damaged) accurately matches with the observations on all four test routes. Furthermore, all the junctions and their shape are identified accurately, and the time-varying junction regulation is inferred with the accuracy of 85% and 95.45% on the two test routes. These results are encouraging and demonstrate the feasibility of using opportunistically collected unlabeled bus trajectories for large-scale road network monitoring.

expanded_papers.title: TS-YOLO: a lightweight real-time traffic sign detector in resource-constrained environments
expanded_papers.abstract: Traffic sign detection is essential for ensuring driving safety in autonomous vehicles and advanced driver-assistance systems, particularly under limited computational resources and challenging real-world conditions such as extreme weather, occlusion, and diverse viewing angles. To address the trade-off between detection accuracy and computational efficiency, we propose TS-YOLO, a lightweight, real-time detector built on You Only Look Once version 8 (YOLOv8). TS-YOLO integrates a Repetition Cross Stage Partial (RepCSP) feature-enhancement module and a set of targeted training strategies designed to improve small-object recognition and to handle various challenging scenarios. Experimental results on the CCTSDB2021 dataset show that TS-YOLO achieves 98.4% mean Average Precision (mAP)@0.5 and 79.1% mAP@0.95, with an inference time of only 0.6 ms per frame on embedded hardware. Compared to state-of-the-art methods—including Faster Region-based Convolutional Neural Network (Faster R-CNN), Efficient Object Detection (EfficientDet), and the original YOLOv8—TS-YOLO demonstrates superior accuracy and faster inference. Further robustness evaluations confirm consistent performance across adverse weather (fog, rain, low light), various road types (urban, rural, unstructured), and challenging object characteristics (scale variation, occlusion, irregular shapes). These findings suggest that TS-YOLO is a practical, high-performance solution for traffic sign detection in resource-constrained, complex driving environments.

expanded_papers.title: A Survey of Data Augmentation Techniques for Traffic Visual Elements
expanded_papers.abstract: Highlights What are the main finding? We propose a structured taxonomy specifically for the enhancement of traffic visual elements data, integrating techniques such as image transformation, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Diffusion Models, and composite methods. We construct a comprehensive cross-comparison benchmark encompassing nearly 40 datasets and 10 evaluation metrics, which systematically reveals the performance of different augmentation strategies across key metrics including accuracy, mean average precision (mAP), and robustness. We demonstrate the capability of emerging generative paradigms, particularly diffusion models and multimodal composite models, in representing rare driving scenarios, and analyzes their trade-offs between computational cost and semantic consistency. What are the implications of the main findings? This paper systematically consolidates diverse data augmentation strategies within the domain of traffic visual elements, thereby charting a forward-looking roadmap for researchers engaged in developing perception models. This paper employs a multi-layered analytical framework to systematically link augmentation strategies to real-world performance outcomes, thereby providing methodological guidance for future evaluation and research. This paper identifies the enhancement of data reliability and cross-domain transferability in intelligent transportation systems as a critical future direction, which is paramount for the successful deployment of autonomous driving technologies. Abstract Autonomous driving is a cornerstone of intelligent transportation systems, where visual elements such as traffic signs, lights, and pedestrians are critical for safety and decision-making. Yet, existing datasets often lack diversity, underrepresent rare scenarios, and suffer from class imbalance, which limits the robustness of object detection models. While earlier reviews have examined general image enhancement, a systematic analysis of dataset augmentation for traffic visual elements remains lacking. This paper presents a comprehensive investigation of enhancement techniques tailored for transportation datasets. It pursues three objectives: establishing a classification framework for autonomous driving scenarios, assessing performance gains from augmentation methods on tasks such as detection and classification, and providing practical insights to guide dataset improvement in both research and industry. Four principal approaches are analyzed, including image transformation, GAN-based generation, diffusion models, and composite methods, with discussion of their strengths, limitations, and emerging strategies. Nearly 40 traffic-related datasets and 10 evaluation metrics are reviewed to support benchmarking. Results show that augmentation improves robustness under challenging conditions, with hybrid methods often yielding the best outcomes. Nonetheless, key challenges remain, including computational costs, unstable GAN training, and limited rare scene data. Future work should prioritize lightweight models, richer semantic context, specialized datasets, and scalable, efficient strategies.

innovation_points.description: 引入多尺度特征融合模块（如ASFF或自适应特征融合）和上下文增强模块（如空洞卷积或RFB）以提升复杂场景下交通标志的检测鲁棒性。
innovation_points.baseline_used: CR-YOLOv8: Multiscale Object Detection in Traffic Sign Images
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 在YOLOv8的颈部网络输出后添加ASFF模块，对不同尺度的特征图进行自适应加权融合。2. 在骨干网络最后阶段插入空洞卷积层（rate=2,3），扩大感受野。3. 在GTSDB数据集上训练，使用mAP@0.5:0.95和推理时间评估。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: CR-YOLOv8: Multiscale Object Detection in Traffic Sign Images

innovation_points.description: 采用无锚框检测头（如FCOS或CenterNet）替换YOLOv8的锚框检测头，并引入单级特征图（如仅使用P4）以简化结构，同时结合自适应图像增强预处理。
innovation_points.baseline_used: A Robust Real-Time Anchor-Free Traffic Sign Detector With One-Level Feature
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 移除YOLOv8的锚框检测头，替换为FCOS检测头（中心度+分类+回归分支）。2. 仅保留颈部网络中的P4特征图（16x下采样），并调整检测头输入。3. 在数据预处理阶段加入自适应直方图均衡化（CLAHE）和伽马校正。4. 在TT100K数据集上训练，对比mAP和参数量。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: A Robust Real-Time Anchor-Free Traffic Sign Detector With One-Level Feature

stitching_plan.module_c: 小目标检测头来自TSD-YOLO论文
stitching_steps: 1. 复现YOLOv8 baseline：使用Ultralytics官方代码库，在TT100K数据集上训练，记录mAP@0.5和FPS。
2. 提取模块B：从YOLO-BS论文中实现DCNv2模块，替换YOLOv8骨干网络最后两个阶段的3x3卷积。
3. 提取模块C：从TSD-YOLO论文中实现小目标检测头，在颈部网络增加160x160特征图输出和对应检测头。
4. 拼接测试：将两个模块集成到YOLOv8中，在相同数据集上训练，使用mAP@0.5和FPS对比baseline。
risk_notes: DCNv2可能增加训练难度和显存占用，需调整学习率和batch size。
小目标检测头会增加计算量，可能降低FPS，需平衡精度和速度。
两个模块同时引入可能导致梯度冲突，建议先单独验证每个模块的效果。

experiment_checklist: 数据集划分与预处理一致
所有对比实验使用相同训练超参数
消融实验每次只改变一个变量
记录每次实验的随机种子
验证集与测试集严格分离
对比论文使用官方或复现的最佳配置
结果表格包含均值与标准差

optimization_paths.direction: 多尺度特征融合与注意力机制改进
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 提升小目标交通标志检测精度，缓解尺度变化问题
action_items: 在YOLOv8骨干网络引入可变形卷积或空洞卷积增强多尺度感受野
添加通道-空间混合注意力模块（如CBAM或SE）聚焦关键特征
设计特征金字塔跨层连接策略，强化小目标语义信息

optimization_paths.direction: 轻量化网络与嵌入式部署优化
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 降低模型参数量和计算量，满足实时性要求
action_items: 采用深度可分离卷积替换标准卷积，减少参数量
使用知识蒸馏或模型剪枝技术压缩YOLOv8
针对嵌入式设备（如Jetson Nano）进行TensorRT加速优化

optimization_paths.direction: 数据增强与域适应策略
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 增强模型在复杂环境（光照、遮挡、天气）下的鲁棒性
action_items: 引入Mosaic、MixUp等数据增强方法模拟多样场景
使用GAN生成恶劣天气（雨、雾、雪）下的交通标志样本
结合自监督预训练或域随机化技术适应不同数据集

degradation_paths.path: 退化为传统图像处理+机器学习方法（如HOG+SVM）
degradation_paths.trade_off: 牺牲检测精度和泛化能力，但计算资源需求极低，可快速验证

degradation_paths.path: 退化为仅检测静态交通标志（忽略动态场景）
degradation_paths.trade_off: 简化问题，但无法满足自动驾驶实际需求，论文创新性降低

degradation_paths.path: 退化为仅使用公开数据集（如TT100K）而不进行实地采集
degradation_paths.trade_off: 减少数据获取成本，但可能缺乏场景多样性，导致过拟合

risk_mitigation: 优先使用公开数据集（如TT100K、GTSDB）进行实验，确保基线可复现
设置多组消融实验，量化每个改进模块的贡献，避免创新点堆砌
提前规划嵌入式部署验证，若计算资源不足可退化为轻量级模型
与导师定期沟通，确保研究方向与实验室现有资源匹配
准备备选退化方案（如仅检测静态标志），以防实验效果不达预期

dimension_scores.reason: 创新点明确针对交通标志检测中的小目标、不规则形状和轻量化需求，与现有baseline的改进方向一致，gap真实存在。
baseline选择合理，均为YOLOv8改进的交通标志检测方法，但未提供数据集和代码仓库，方法学严谨性略受影响。
baseline有3篇（≥2），但parallel和dataset均缺失，未达到PASS标准，存在数据收集风险。
工作包与创新点基本对应，但问题描述缺失，论证连贯性一般。
叙事部分几乎空白，缺乏自洽性，存在过度宣传风险（如未说明数据集来源）。

risks_identified: 无公开数据集和代码仓库，需自行收集和标注数据，可能影响实验可复现性。
叙事部分缺失，需补充问题定义和模型命名以增强论证连贯性。
轻量化部署部分未明确嵌入式设备型号和性能指标，存在实现不确定性。
